docs.title: Welcome to MkDocs
docs.text: ChatPointsTTV is a Bukkit plugin that helps you to create interactions between your Twitch stream and your Minecraft world. Set up your own rewards in exchange of channel points, follows, Bits, subs and more! Spawn mobs, give items or run any command when an event is triggered. There's a whole range of use cases and possibilities to connect with your audience!

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: This plugin is controlled by the /twitch command followed by one of the following arguments:

docs.title: Link
docs.text: Links the plugin to a Twitch account. If a key-based credential is set it will use it. Otherwise will prompt the user to log in through a browser (only works if the user logs in with the same machine as the server). You can specify the method to use through the optional [method] parameter. Valid options: key, browser

docs.title: Unlink
docs.text: Disconnects from Twitch and therefore, disables the main functionality. You may need to log in again if you used the browser method.

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Displays an information message about connection status, plugin version, listened an live channels and connected account.

docs.title: Reload
docs.text: Restarts the plugin internally without having to restart or reload the whole server. It will also disconnect you from Twitch, and you will have to connect again using the link command.

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
• /twitch test <event type> <...> Sends a custom test event locally. It is useful for testing your configuration.

    Valid event types: - channelpoints - cheer - follow - raid - sub - subgift

docs.title: Channel Points
docs.text:
Sends a custom Channel Points Redemption event. You can input the chatter and streamer user names, as well as adding a user input message. Syntax: /twitch test channelpoints <chatter username> <streamer username> <reward name> [user input]

If you have a reward name that contains whitespaces you should enclose it with double quotes

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
/twitch test channelpoints gospelbg rubius "Blow me up!"

/twitch test channelpoints gospelbg <> "Rename my current item" "The Un-aliver"

docs.title: Cheer
docs.text: Sends a custom Cheer event. You can set a custom amount of Bits, as well as the cheerer and the streamer username. Syntax: /twitch test cheer <cheerer username> <streamer username> <bits>

docs.title: Follow
docs.text: Sends a custom Follow event. You Syntax: /twitch test follow <follower username> <streamer username>
